description: Agent-facing Type-4 adversarial coverage matrix. It selects co-evolution work across engine, oracle, proof facts, and performance.

cells.title: Python multi-clause comprehension equals nested builder loop and JS flatMap/map
cells.evidence: PR #66 added HoFKind::FlatMap, removed the Python Raw fallback, and verified the focused flat-map corpus with zero false merges.

cells.title: Rust filter_map converges with filter+map and option-producing loops
cells.evidence: Rust .filter_map(|x| if p { Some(v) } else { None }) now lowers to HoF(FilterMap) and value-graphs to the same filtered Map value as Python comprehensions, JS filter().map(), and Rust Vec::new()/push guarded builders. Hard negatives split mapped None payloads, changed Some values, and truthy filtering after emitted 0. Successor audit: richer Rust match/and_then option shapes and wrapped Some(None)-style payloads are covered by hof.filter_map.rich_option_shapes; future option helper gaps should attach there or to an oracle-only effect boundary.
cells.agent_task: Monitor the narrow Rust if-expression filter_map and guarded-builder slice. New option-shape gaps should attach to hof.filter_map.rich_option_shapes unless they are oracle-only effect boundaries.
required_gates: cargo run -p nose-cli -- scan bench/type4/adversarial/cases/hof_filter_map --mode semantic --min-lines 1 --min-size 1 --format json --top 0

cells.title: Filter-map rich option-producing shapes beyond simple Some/None if
cells.evidence: Rust match-guard option callbacks and pure Some(x).and_then(...) helper chains now value-graph to the same filtered Map as Python/TypeScript filter+map when they preserve the predicate and emitted value. Wrapped Some(None) is modeled as an emitted Null payload, not absence, and stays distinct from mapped x*2. Effectful callbacks remain fail-closed and covered by hof_effectful_lambda. Successor audit: no new cell was added because the remaining unmodeled option helper/effect boundaries are already represented by this cell's rule/case boundaries.
cells.agent_task: Monitor richer Rust option-producing filter_map shapes. If a new helper chain is behavior-equal but under-merged, merge it into this covered cell's successor evidence unless it requires new oracle effect modeling.

cells.title: Aggregates over flat-map streams converge with nested reduction loops
cells.evidence: Pure FlatMap aggregates now consume the emitted inner Map value explicitly instead of treating FlatMap's [outer, inner] args like filtered Map's [contrib, pred] layout. Nested reduction loops that carry an accumulator through an inner Reduce converge with sum/max/any flat-map generators and JS flatMap().reduce(); wrong seed, nested-list aggregation, changed any predicate, and outer-cardinality-dependent aggregates stay split. Successor audit: filtered Sum/Reduce FlatMap aggregates and method terminal predicates are covered by hof.flat_map.filtered_aggregate_bridge; filtered nested early-return any/all loops are covered by hof.flat_map.filtered_any_early_return_loop.
cells.agent_task: Monitor pure FlatMap aggregate consumers. Filtered FlatMap aggregate or terminal-method gaps should attach to hof.flat_map.filtered_aggregate_bridge.
required_gates: cargo run -p nose-cli -- scan bench/type4/adversarial/cases/flat_map_aggregate --mode semantic --min-lines 1 --min-size 1 --format json --top 0

cells.title: Filtered FlatMap aggregate consumers preserve carried predicates
cells.evidence: Filtered FlatMap Sum/Reduce consumers now carry outer and inner filter predicates into the reduction identity branch, so Python filtered flat-map sums converge with equivalent nested guarded reduction loops and JS filtered flatMap().reduce(). JS method-terminal .some() over a filtered FlatMap also converges with the equivalent Python filtered any generator while changed outer, inner, and terminal predicates stay split. Successor audit: filtered nested early-return any/all loops are covered by hof.flat_map.filtered_any_early_return_loop.
cells.agent_task: Monitor filtered FlatMap aggregate consumers. Predicate-carrying Sum/Reduce, method-terminal Any/All, and nested early-return any/all loops are covered.

cells.title: Filtered FlatMap any/all loops converge with method/generator aggregates
cells.evidence: Bool-proven guarded identity Phi contributions now canonicalize to the same logical predicate shape as nested early-return existence/universal loops, and bool-proven logical AND/OR chains flatten across grouping. Python filtered any/all generators, JS filtered flatMap().some()/every(), and equivalent Python nested guarded early-return loops converge while changed terminal predicates and nested-list boundaries stay split. Successor audit: no new FlatMap successor cell was added; the remaining top frontier is the existing HoF value-graph perf budget.
cells.agent_task: Monitor filtered FlatMap nested early-return any/all loops. New predicate-carrying aggregate gaps should attach to hof.flat_map.filtered_aggregate_bridge unless they need a distinct oracle or performance boundary.

cells.title: Java Stream.flatMap converges with shared FlatMap HoF
cells.evidence: Pure Java Arrays.stream(xs).flatMap(x -> Arrays.stream(ys).map(...)) now converges with Python multi-clause comprehension through the shared FlatMap HoF. The oracle interprets Java expression-body stream lambdas; map-returning-stream remains nested, and effectful callbacks are observable rather than assumed pure. Successor audit: the closed gap was the Java stream oracle/lambda blocker; no new successor cell was added because aggregate/terminal FlatMap consumers are already tracked by hof.flat_map.aggregate_bridge.
cells.agent_task: Monitor pure Java stream flatMap coverage. New aggregate or terminal-consumer gaps should attach to hof.flat_map.aggregate_bridge unless they require a Java-only oracle boundary.

cells.title: Clamp-shaped min/max compositions converge under proven bounds
cells.evidence: Proof-backed integer min/max clamp compositions now canonicalize to a Clamp value only when a unique x/lo/hi candidate has integer-domain facts and a lo <= hi proof. Focused tests cover exiting inverse guards, ordered literal bounds, missing/non-exiting guards, swapped bounds, wrong nesting, and float domains. Surface bridge forms are covered by numeric.clamp.ternary_library_bridge.
cells.agent_task: Monitor the landed min/max composition slice. New surface-specific regressions should attach to numeric.clamp_surface_bridge unless they require a separate proof/oracle rule.

cells.title: Two-comparison and library clamp forms bridge to proven Clamp values
cells.evidence: Two-comparison lower-first and upper-first clamp ternaries now bridge to the proof-backed integer Clamp value, and Rust numeric .clamp bridges to literal ordered bounds plus guarded Rust x.max(lo).min(hi) under the same lo <= hi proof. Missing proofs, swapped bounds, float domains, unproven parameter .clamp calls, and custom clamp methods stay split. Successor audit: the closed gap was the surface bridge left by numeric.clamp.minmax_composition; the newly confirmed method-name-only risk is covered by clamp_method_name_only_boundary, so no successor cell was added.
cells.agent_task: Monitor the landed ternary/library clamp bridge. New clamp surface gaps should first test the existing integer-domain, lo<=hi proof, and method-receiver boundaries before adding another cell.

cells.title: Cross-file immutable map/default lookup facts
cells.evidence: Python sibling `from module import LOOKUP` facts resolve to a cloned literal RHS only when the provider has one safe unmutated literal binding and the import is unambiguous. Focused tests and fixtures prove imported/local map-default convergence while wrong-map, provider mutation, and importer mutation stay split. The earlier JS/TS/Java/Rust successor is now covered by map_default.non_python_import_bridge.
cells.agent_task: Monitor the landed Python sibling-import slice. Broader non-Python imported module/factory resolution is covered by map_default.non_python_import_bridge.
required_gates: cargo run -p nose-cli -- scan bench/type4/adversarial/cases/map_default_cross_file --mode semantic --min-lines 1 --min-size 1 --format json --top 0

cells.title: Non-Python imported module map-default bindings converge when provenance is proven
cells.evidence: JS/TS named imports from unambiguous sibling map exports, Java static imports from class static Map.of fields, and Rust use-imported const entry arrays now resolve to safe cloned provider RHS values. Focused tests and map_default_non_python_import fixtures prove convergence with local literal map-default lookup while JS provider/importer mutation and JS/Java/Rust wrong-map imports stay split. Successor audit: the closed gap was non-Python imported map-default provenance; the newly surfaced Rust const-entry fixture need is recorded as map_default_imported_rust_const_literal, and no extra successor cell was added because unsupported coordinates still fail closed under proof.collection_provenance.
cells.agent_task: Monitor the landed non-Python import bridge. New map-default import gaps should first test unambiguous provider identity, mutation, shadowing, and wrong-map boundaries before adding another cell.

cells.title: Oracle keeps HoF and iterator error semantics in step with engine canons
cells.evidence: FilterMap-style option iterator semantics now have oracle coverage: lambda errors propagate, empty collections skip callbacks, Null drops, falsey values remain emitted, scalar FlatMap callback results Err, closure captures work, and effectful callbacks stay observable.
cells.agent_task: Monitor future HoF/iterator canons against the landed oracle cases before broadening engine rules.

cells.title: HoF value-graph growth stays bounded as iterator coverage expands
cells.evidence: Iterator/HOF coverage can increase fingerprint DAG size. A reusable smoke budget should exist before accepting broad chain-fusion work.
cells.agent_task: Add a compact perf smoke that records runtime and value-node/fingerprint-size budgets for representative HoF chains; use it before broadening iterator fusion.
